name: Setna Khaemweset
description: Setna Khaemweset, a prince of ancient Egypt and son of Ramesses II, is renowned as a skilled magician, scholar, and scribe. He is often depicted as a learned figure deeply engrossed in the ancient texts and secrets of the past. His most famous tale revolves around his quest to obtain the Book of Thoth, a powerful grimoire said to contain unimaginable knowledge and magical abilities. Setna's ambition led him to Nekheb, where he confronted the ghost of Prince Naneferkaptah, the book's original owner, in a dangerous game of Senet. To win the book, Setna endured trials, including the death of his family, before ultimately proving his worthiness through cunning and resilience. After obtaining the Book of Thoth, Setna used its magic wisely, but eventually realized its power was too great for any mortal to possess safely. He returned the book to Naneferkaptah's tomb, restoring balance and demonstrating the importance of respecting ancient knowledge and the boundaries between the mortal and spiritual realms. Setna's story serves as a cautionary tale about the allure of forbidden knowledge and the consequences of unchecked ambition, while also celebrating the pursuit of wisdom and the importance of humility.
subtitle: Magician, Scholar, and Prince of Egypt
achievements: Returning the Book of Thoth to its rightful place

enemies.name: Forces unleashed by the Book of Thoth

keyMyths.title: The Quest for the Book of Thoth
keyMyths.description: Setna Khaemweset seeks the Book of Thoth, a grimoire containing immense magical power, encountering the ghost of Prince Naneferkaptah who guards it. He plays a game of Senet with the ghost, risking everything for the book's knowledge.
keyMyths.source: The Story of Setna Khaemwas and Si-Osire

keyMyths.title: The Game of Senet
keyMyths.description: Setna challenges Naneferkaptah to a game of Senet to win the Book of Thoth. He uses his magical abilities and wit to overcome the ghost's challenges, but pays a heavy price, experiencing the death of his family to secure his victory.
keyMyths.source: The Story of Setna Khaemwas and Si-Osire

keyMyths.title: The Return of the Book
keyMyths.description: Realizing the danger of the Book of Thoth, Setna returns it to Naneferkaptah's tomb, learning a lesson about the limits of mortal knowledge and the importance of respecting the boundaries between the living and the dead. This act restores balance and peace.
keyMyths.source: The Story of Setna Khaemwas and Si-Osire

sources.author: Unknown
sources.work: The Story of Setna Khaemwas and Si-Osire
sources.description: An ancient Egyptian tale detailing Setna's adventures.
sources.text: An ancient Egyptian tale detailing Setna's adventures.
